report_name: Critical Unauthenticated RCE Flaw Found in WordPress Core

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: The vulnerability CVE-2026-63030 allows an unauthenticated attacker to trigger remote code execution by sending a specially crafted request to the WordPress REST API batch processing endpoint.

attack_patterns.name: Server Software Component: Web Shell
attack_patterns.description: A potential impact of exploiting CVE-2026-63030 is the deployment of web shells for persistent access, often as PHP files in the /wp-content/uploads/ directory.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-63030
vulnerabilities.description: A critical unauthenticated remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability in the WordPress Core REST API batch processing endpoint, allowing an attacker to execute arbitrary code on a vulnerable website without authentication.

indicators.name: WordPress Core CVE-2026-63030 RCE Attempt
indicators.pattern:
title: WordPress Core CVE-2026-63030 RCE Attempt
id: 9c5b1a3d-8e4f-4b2a-9c1d-6a3b2c1d0e9f
status: experimental
description: Detects POST requests to the WordPress REST API batch endpoint, which may indicate an attempt to exploit CVE-2026-63030.
references:
  - https://www.rapid7.com/blog/post/etr-cve-2026-63030-wp2shell-a-critical-remote-code-execution-vulnerability-in-wordpress-core/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-18
tags:
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.initial_access
  - cve.2026.63030
logsource:
  category: webserver
detection:
  selection:
    cs-method: 'POST'
    cs-uri-stem|contains: '/wp-json/v1/batch'
  condition: selection
level: high
indicators.description: This indicator detects attempts to exploit CVE-2026-63030 by monitoring for an unusual volume of POST requests to the WordPress REST API batch endpoint.

indicators.name: Potential WordPress Web Shell Creation
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential WordPress Web Shell Creation
id: f8a7b6c5-d4e3-4f2a-1b0c-9d8e7f6a5b4c
status: experimental
description: Monitors for the creation of non-image files (e.g., .php, .sh) in the WordPress uploads directory, which could be a sign of a successful RCE like CVE-2026-63030.
references:
  - https://www.rapid7.com/blog/post/etr-cve-2026-63030-wp2shell-a-critical-remote-code-execution-vulnerability-in-wordpress-core/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-18
tags:
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.persistence
  - cve.2026.63030
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|contains: '/wp-content/uploads/'
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '.php'
      - '.sh'
  condition: selection
level: high
indicators.description: This indicator detects the creation of suspicious files in the WordPress uploads directory, a common post-exploitation technique for vulnerabilities like CVE-2026-63030.
